fields.caption: Profit Ratio
fields.tableau_formula:
IF MIN([City]) = "Oswego"
THEN ABS(SUM([Profit])/SUM([Sales]))
ELSE SUM([Profit])/SUM([Sales])
END

fields.caption: New colour
fields.tableau_formula:
IF [Calculation_1368249927221915648] < 0  THEN IF MIN([Path])= 0 THEN -1 ELSE 0 END -MIN([Path])
ELSE IF MIN([Path])= 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END + MIN([Path])
END

fields.caption: Angle
fields.tableau_formula:
RADIANS( (MIN([Path]))* (360 / 100))

*

CASE [Calculation_1600185310577041417]
WHEN FALSE THEN 1
ELSE -1
END

fields.caption: Pie Slice Calc
fields.tableau_formula:
If MIN([Path]) = 1 THEN [Calculation_1600185310575546376]
ELSEIF MIN([Path]) = 2 THEN 1-[Calculation_1600185310575546376]
END

fields.caption: Pie Tooltip Value
fields.tableau_formula:
CASE TRUE
WHEN MIN([Path])=1 THEN [Calculation_1368249927221915648]
ELSE 0
END

fields.caption: MP - Last Point
fields.tableau_formula:
MAKEPOINT(

CASE TRUE
WHEN MIN([Path]) = MIN([Calculation_1498572842631897090]) THEN [Calculation_1508987416696180742]
END

,

CASE TRUE
WHEN MIN([Path]) = MIN([Calculation_1498572842631897090]) THEN [X (copy)_1508987416696676360]
END

)

fields.caption: MP - First Point
fields.tableau_formula:
MAKEPOINT(

CASE TRUE
WHEN MIN([Path]) = 0 THEN [Calculation_1508987416696180742]
END

,

CASE TRUE
WHEN MIN([Path]) = 0 THEN [X (copy)_1508987416696676360]
END

)

fields.caption: MP - Needle
fields.tableau_formula:
MAKEPOINT(

CASE TRUE
WHEN MIN([Path]) = MIN([Calculation_1498572842631897090]) THEN [Calculation_1508987416696180742]
WHEN MIN([Path]) = 0 THEN 0
END

,

CASE TRUE
WHEN MIN([Path]) = MIN([Calculation_1498572842631897090]) THEN [X (copy)_1508987416696676360]
WHEN MIN([Path]) = 0 THEN 0
END

)

fields.caption: MP - 100s
fields.tableau_formula:
MAKEPOINT(

CASE TRUE
WHEN MIN([Path]) IN (100,200,300,400,500) THEN [Calculation_1508987416696180742]
END

,

CASE TRUE
WHEN MIN([Path]) IN (100,200,300,400,500) THEN [X (copy)_1508987416696676360]
END

)

fields.caption: MP - Spiral Overlay
fields.tableau_formula:
MAKEPOINT(

IF [Calculation_1508987416698376201]
THEN
[Calculation_1508987416696180742]
ELSE NULL
END
,

IF [Calculation_1508987416698376201]
THEN
[X (copy)_1508987416696676360]
ELSE NULL
END

)

fields.caption: MP - Axis guides
fields.tableau_formula:
MAKEPOINT(
CASE [Path]
WHEN 1 THEN 0  //Centre
WHEN 2 THEN 0  // Up
WHEN 3 THEN 0  // Down
WHEN 4 THEN 0  //Centre
WHEN 5 THEN 2.9  // Right
WHEN 6 THEN -2.9 // Left
END

,

CASE [Path]
WHEN 1 THEN 0  //Centre
WHEN 2 THEN 2.9  // Up
WHEN 3 THEN -2.9  // Down
WHEN 4 THEN 0  //Centre
WHEN 5 THEN 0  // Right
WHEN 6 THEN 0 // Left
END

)

worksheets.name: Banding
worksheets.title_text:
... with bandingÆ
Gives a sense of a greater extent of the values is possible.

worksheets.name: Beyond 100%
worksheets.title_text:
Satellite Chart beyond 100%Æ
Depth and quantity of colour draws the attention and focus on those particularly significant.

worksheets.name: Doughnuts
worksheets.title_text:
A major issue with a standard Doughnut Chart or Pie ChartÆ
Doughnut charts and Pie charts are recognisable and work well for percentages - until those percentages move past 100%, then they become obsolete as a visualisation.Æ
Example below:

worksheets.name: Pointer needle
worksheets.title_text:
... with pointer needleÆ
Provides a visual focus onto the point of importance, the end point.  Helping the user with a particularly unusual visual.

worksheets.name: Snake
worksheets.title_text:
... snake line with prominent 100% indicatorsÆ
The thicker snake line gives even more emphasis on the colour depth and quantity.  The 100% indicators allow for a quicker understanding of the size of the metric.

children.text_html: Satellite or orbital charts are an unusual visualisation type that occupy the same visualisation space as your standard pie chart.  The difference is, a satellite chart uses a circle marker to denote the position around the radial that the value represents, rather than a slice of a pie.  That circle is connected to a line that arcs around from the top to the circle marker.

The satellite chart can really only showcase a single value at a time, whereas a pie chart or doughnut chart can show multiple values as a part-to-whole solution.  That doesn't mean one is better than the other, just they are different visuals that are valid options to the visualisation developer.

Design & method:Æ
Data: Æ
Bluesky:Æ
LinkedIn:Æ

A doughnut chart is essentially a pie chart with a hole in it.  It visualises a single or multiple slices of a pie, cut at various angles.  However, doughnut charts when hole in the centre is large and a small slice of the pie is left visible, actually can be perceived as a bar that runs around the outside of the doughnut.  Both concepts of reading the chart are acceptable.

What if we could take the concept of a doughnut chart and extend the bar that runs around the outside past the 100% mark and start to add layers as the value increases beyond 100%?  We would create a spiral with multiple rings to it.  Each full ring representing 100% adding up together.

To make this easier to read, we could alter the chart from a doughnut to a satellite chart.  As explained above, the concepts between these charts are similar, the advantage a satellite chart would give us here, is the ability to follow the orbit around multiple rings.  This will allow for an easier comprehension of what the chart is portraying.

theme.source_note: Tableau has no single global theme file - aggregated from per-worksheet mark-color formats; treat as a starting palette, not an authoritative theme to clone.

limitations_encountered.item: MAKEPOINT spatial fields (MP - Spiral, MP - First/Last Point, MP - Needle, MP - 100s, MP - Spiral Overlay, MP - Zero, MP - Titles, MP - Axis guides)
limitations_encountered.issue: Power BI has no native point-geometry type equivalent to Tableau MAKEPOINT plotted on a Map. Capability gap. Reproduced by materialising the underlying computed coordinate columns Spiral[X]/Spiral[Y] and driving a native scatterChart (X=Spiral[X], Y=Spiral[Y]) as a per-City small-multiple. Boolean helper columns Is First Point (Path=0), Is Last Point (Path=Max Path), Is 100s Marker (Path IN 100,200,300,400) replace the MP-First/Last/100s overlay layers for conditional formatting/needle endpoints. MP-Zero(0,0), MP-Titles(3,3) and MP-Axis guides (6-row CASE scaffold) are static annotation layers left to the report builder. Fidelity: spiral shape reproduced exactly; Tableau Map background/geographic-projection semantics are not applicable and intentionally dropped.

limitations_encountered.item: Spiral densification (Orders 1=1 Model cross join over Path axis)
limitations_encountered.issue: Tableau densifies each city's spiral across the Model table's Path domain (0..400) via a 1=1 object-model join (a table-calc/INDEX-style scaffold). Materialised as calculated table Spiral = CROSSJOIN(City, Model[Path]) = 542 cities x 401 paths = 217,342 rows. The per-city ratio family (Profit Ratio, Abs Profit Ratio, Pie Colour, Decimals, Max Path) is denormalised onto Spiral via SELECTCOLUMNS so the row-level trig columns are self-contained (no cross-table RELATED inside calculated columns - deliberate robustness choice).

Nested FIXED LOD { FIXED [City]: SUM({ FIXED [City],[Path]: IF SpiralFilter THEN 1 END }) } - 1 counts path rows under the spiral filter minus one. Translated to calculated column Max Path = MIN(INT([Abs Profit Ratio]*100), 400) = floor(AbsRatio*100) capped at the Model scaffold maximum (400). Ground-truthed: Houston->15, Oswego->166.

Feeds the row-level Radius = [Parameter Radius] + [Path] calc. A live Power BI what-if parameter cannot drive a calculated column (it would require re-expressing every coordinate as a measure over a disconnected slicer, which changes the scatter binding model). Baked at its default value 500. Fidelity ceiling: radius is fixed; interactive radius tuning from the Tableau parameter is not reproduced. Other parameters (Top Customers=5, Profit Bin Size=200, Model=5) are unused by the spiral worksheets and omitted.

limitations_encountered.item: Profit Ratio - Oswego special-case (possible source quirk)
limitations_encountered.issue: Source formula hardcodes IF MIN([City]) = 'Oswego' THEN ABS(SUM(Profit)/SUM(Sales)) ELSE SUM(Profit)/SUM(Sales). Only this one city is ABS'd, which flips its Pie Colour (Profit Ratio<0) to False and reverses its spiral winding direction. Almost certainly an author test artifact. Translated FAITHFULLY (not silently normalised) and flagged here for customer confirmation. Also: source caption typo 'Abs Proit Ratio' cleaned to 'Abs Profit Ratio'.

People (Region, 4 rows) and Returns (Order ID, 800 rows) hyper tables are unreferenced by any of the 5 spiral worksheets and were omitted. Report/vestigial fields not modelled: Profit (bin); Manufacturer (a rename aliasing [Profit]); the Doughnuts MIN(1) pie-wedge constant; Number of Records. The four Tableau relationship-anchor pseudo-columns (data_type=table) are excluded per convention.

Extract-based (.hyper) source. Orders (10,194 rows) imported from data/ds.sample_superstore.csv (columns Order ID, City, Region, Sales, Profit). The Model Path densification scaffold (401 rows, Path 0..400) was exported from the .hyper Model table to data/ds.model.csv and imported. Import mode with explicit en-US culture on Table.TransformColumnTypes. No live connection retained.

limitations_encountered.item: Validation coverage (no live engine in batch)
limitations_encountered.issue: Structural: TOM TmdlSerializer.DeserializeDatabaseFromFolder deserialises cleanly (same parser Power BI Desktop uses); 46/46 DAX bracket references resolve; 0 measures (measure-name uniqueness and measure/column collision checks trivially hold). Numeric: X/Y verified two independent ways in Python against the source CSV (agree to 2.6e-15) for 6 probes - Houston P0/P15, New York City P10, Oswego P0/P166, Naperville P5. A live EVALUATE was not run: no Power BI Desktop/PBIP is open in this headless parallel batch and Tableau-rendered coordinate values are not persisted in the .twb (coordinates are deterministic given the CSV + formulas, which is what the Python ground-truth computes).

limitations_encountered.item: Copilot readiness (AI-prep pass) - service-only AI features not committable from TMDL
limitations_encountered.issue: AI-prep completed: business-meaning descriptions added to all 4 tables and all 33 columns (meaning + unit/grain led, front-loaded for the 200-char DAX Copilot window), plus domain enumerations for the low-cardinality categorical Orders[Region] = {Central, East, South, West} and every boolean flag (One of: True, False). NOT applied because they have no stable Git/TMDL file contract today and must be set later in the Power BI/Fabric service: the linguistic AI data schema + AI instructions (LSDL), Copilot 'verified answers', and the Approved-for-Copilot / semantic-model indexing settings. Persisted via TOM ExportToTmdlFolder (cosmetic quoting/whitespace normalized model-wide; DAX, lineageTags and structure preserved; re-validated: deserialize clean, 46/46 bracket refs resolve, 4/4 tables + 33/33 columns described).

limitations_encountered.item: Scatter small-multiples gap - one satellite per view (single-city slicer substitution)
limitations_encountered.issue: Tableau's satellite/spiral technique renders one city's spiral per worksheet and can trellis many cities as small multiples. Power BI's native scatterChart has NO Rows/small-multiples role (confirmed via `powerbi-report-author catalog describe scatterChart`), and its Category well (max 1) is consumed by Path to trace the spiral, so City cannot also be trellised in the same visual. Reproduced as a single-satellite view driven by a single-select City slicer (default 'Abilene', the largest-magnitude spiral at -270% profit ratio = 2.7 rings). Preserves the author's stated intent ('a satellite chart can really only showcase a single value at a time') and is more interactive than a static trellis, at the cost of not showing all 542 cities at once.

limitations_encountered.item: No connecting polyline for the continuous 'snake' spiral trail
limitations_encountered.issue: scatterChart plots markers only; it cannot draw an ordered line connecting Path 0->400, so the continuous 'snake' trail is approximated by the density of the 401 plotted points (which read as a near-continuous curve) plus accent markers at each 100th point (Is 100s Marker overlay). A truly stroked spiral line is not achievable natively: lineChart cannot plot a 2D polar X/Y coordinate pair. Shape is faithful; line continuity is density-based rather than a stroked path.

limitations_encountered.item: Center-hole annotations (Tableau MP-Titles) not reproduced natively
limitations_encountered.issue: The Tableau infographic places the city name and value as text in each spiral's empty centre via a dual-axis text mark. Native scatterChart has no centre-annotation layer. Conveyed instead through the City slicer and per-chart side descriptions. A per-satellite textbox could add the city label but would be static (not bound to the slicer selection), so it was omitted to keep the view data-driven.

limitations_encountered.item: Square/circular aspect relies on fixed hidden axes - pending live Desktop confirmation
limitations_encountered.issue: Spiral circularity is enforced by fixing both categoryAxis (X) and valueAxis (Y) to start=-2.6, end=2.6 while hiding the axes (show=false). X/Y are origin-symmetric (Radius 500..900 /360 => +-2.5), so independent auto-scaling would stay near-square even if the fixed range were dropped, making severe distortion unlikely. Not visually confirmed in Desktop this session. If a rendered spiral looks elliptical, set both axes show=true (labels can be recoloured to the page background) to force the fixed range.

limitations_encountered.item: Live Power BI Desktop verification deferred (bridge contention)
limitations_encountered.issue: Structural validation passed with 0 errors (`powerbi-report-author validate`) and all 8 distinct Entity.Property references were cross-checked against the semantic model TMDL and resolve. Live Desktop reload+screenshot was deferred: at build time the Desktop bridge had two other in-flight builds attached (airline-alliance-activity WITH unsaved changes, and superstore-sales-performance), and per subagent guidance the single Desktop bridge must not be contended nor a sibling's unsaved instance force-opened. The deliverable is a report-only PBIR folder (no .pbip wrapper) so it cannot be opened standalone without extra scaffolding. Follow-up: open against ../SpiralingSatellites.SemanticModel, refresh, and confirm (a) the scatter traces a continuous spiral for Abilene, (b) ring-band colours cascade light->navy outward, (c) the needle-tip (red) and 100s (gold) overlays align on the base spiral.
